qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Slow partial HTTP/3 POST bodies can starve CoreDNS DoH3 requests
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets the DoH3 HTTP request path (`ServerHTTPS3.ServeHTTP` -> `doh.RequestToMsgWire` -> `io.ReadAll` on POST bodies), while the advisory targets the distinct DoQ stream worker/backlog path in `core/dnsserver/server_quic.go` with blocking `readDOQMessage` framing reads.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's DoQ root cause. The only final qualifying finding is a separate DoH3 slow-body starvation issue on the HTTP/3 request-body read path, not the advisory's DoQ worker-pool backlog and blocking stream-framing read path.
evidence: The advisory describes CoreDNS DoQ in `core/dnsserver/server_quic.go`: accepted QUIC streams can accumulate behind the global worker pool, and active workers can block in `readDOQMessage()` on `io.ReadFull()` while only 1 byte of the 2-byte length prefix has been sent.

Upstream CoreDNS v1.14.3 release notes explicitly call out the advisory-aligned fix as `core: Avoid spawning waiter goroutines when QUIC worker pool is full`, which is specific to the DoQ worker/backlog mechanism.

The final qualifying finding `doh3_slow_post_dos` instead documents `ServerHTTPS3.ServeHTTP` -> `doh.RequestToMsgWire` -> `toMsgWire`, where `io.ReadAll(http.MaxBytesReader(...))` waits for EOF on partial HTTP/3 POST bodies and starves later DoH3 requests.

The DoH3 PoC report verifies timeout of a legitimate follow-up `https://127.0.0.1:8443/dns-query` request under partial POST load, but it does not exercise the advisory's DoQ message-framing code, worker-pool backlog condition, or `readDOQMessage()` path.
